deck_tag_source: DOCUMENTED DEFAULT — hand-authored MileDown approximation, NOT read from the .apkg
outline_source: AAMC MCAT Content Outline (public, official). Foundational Concepts (1-10) and Content Categories (e.g. 1A, 4C) with their short official titles.

content_categories.title: Translational motion, forces, work, energy, and equilibrium in living systems
Importance of fluids for the circulation of blood, gas movement, and gas exchange
Electrochemistry and electrical circuits and their elements
How light and sound interact with matter

matched_by: MileDown::General Chemistry::Atomic Structure and Periodic Table
content_categories.title: Atoms, nuclear decay, electronic structure, and atomic chemical behavior

foundational_concepts.title: Complex living organisms transport materials, sense their environment, process signals, and respond to changes using processes understood in terms of physical principles.

content_categories.title: Unique nature of water and its solutions
Nature of molecules and intermolecular interactions
Separation and purification methods
Structure, function, and reactivity of biologically relevant molecules
Principles of chemical thermodynamics and kinetics

foundational_concepts.title: The principles that govern chemical interactions and reactions form the basis for a broader understanding of the molecular dynamics of living systems.

sections.title: Chemical and Physical Foundations of Biological Systems

sections.note: CARS is a skills-based section: it tests reading comprehension and reasoning over passages, NOT a body of memorizable facts. AAMC publishes no Foundational Concepts or Content Categories for it. It is therefore represented honestly with zero content categories and is EXCLUDED from the content-category coverage denominator. A flashcard deck cannot 'cover' CARS the way it covers a fact-based category; CARS readiness is trained by timed passage practice, not by cards.
sections.title: Critical Analysis and Reasoning Skills (CARS)

content_categories.title: Structure and function of proteins and their constituent amino acids
Transmission of genetic information from the gene to the protein
Transmission of heritable information from generation to generation and the processes that increase genetic diversity
Principles of bioenergetics and fuel molecule metabolism

foundational_concepts.title: Biomolecules have unique properties that determine how they contribute to the structure and function of cells, and how they participate in the processes necessary to maintain life.

content_categories.title: Assemblies of molecules, cells, and groups of cells within single cellular and multicellular organisms
The structure, growth, physiology, and genetics of prokaryotes and viruses
Processes of cell division, differentiation, and specialization

foundational_concepts.title: Highly organized assemblies of molecules, cells, and organs interact to carry out the functions of living organisms.

content_categories.title: Structure and functions of the nervous and endocrine systems and ways these systems coordinate the organ systems
Structure and integrative functions of the main organ systems

foundational_concepts.title: Complex systems of tissues and organs sense the internal and external environments of multicellular organisms, and through integrated functioning, maintain a stable internal environment.

sections.title: Biological and Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems

content_categories.title: Sensing the environment
Making sense of the environment
Responding to the world

foundational_concepts.title: Biological, psychological, and sociocultural factors influence the ways that individuals perceive, think about, and react to the world.

matched_by: MileDown::Psychology::Personality and Biological Basis of Behavior
content_categories.title: Individual influences on behavior
Social processes that influence human behavior
Attitude and behavior change

foundational_concepts.title: Biological, psychological, and sociocultural factors influence behavior and behavior change.

content_categories.title: Self-identity
Social thinking
Social interactions

foundational_concepts.title: Psychological, sociocultural, and biological factors influence the way we think about ourselves and others, as well as how we interact with others.

content_categories.title: Understanding social structure
Demographic characteristics and processes

foundational_concepts.title: Cultural and social differences influence well-being.

content_categories.title: Social inequality

foundational_concepts.title: Social stratification and access to resources influence well-being.

sections.title: Psychological, Social, and Biological Foundations of Behavior
